choiceresponse.label: Across the SHAP, LIME, and Grad‑CAM examples,, which conclusions are supported? (Select all that apply.)

checkboxgroup.choice: A feature can be globally important yet irrelevant for a specific instance.
Local explanations are simply subsets of global explanations.
Global explanations summarize average behavior, while local explanations capture instance-specific reasoning.
If a feature ranks highly globally, it must appear in every local explanation.

div.p: Across SHAP, Grad-CAM, and LIME, we saw that global importance does not guarantee local relevance; global and local explanations tell us different things about how a model behaves. Local explanations reflect instance-specific evidence, while global summaries average over many cases. The two perspectives answer different questions and are not interchangeable.
